text:
Brilliant advice 😂😂

At one point during a game, the coach called one of his 9-year-old baseball players aside
And asked, "Do you understand what cooperation is? What a team is?"
"Yes, coach", replied the little boy. "
Do you understand that what matters is whether we win or lose together as a team?"
The little boy nodded in the affirmative.
"So," the coach continued, "I'm sure you know, when an out is called, you shouldn't argue,
curse the umpire or call him an asshole. Do you understand all that?"
Once more, the small boy nodded in agreement.
The coach continued, "And when I take you out of the game so that another boy gets a chance to play,
it's not a dumb-ass decision or that the coach is a shithead is it?"
"No, coach."
"Good", said the coach. "Now go over there and explain all that to your grandmother.”

🚨🚨Giorgia Meloni on behalf of the Italian state has signed a bilateral security cooperation agreement with Ukrainian President Zelenski.
The agreement is valid for ten years (10 years). The agreement requires Italy to intervene in support of Kiev within 24 hours in the event of a new Moscow attack and to continue to provide economic and military aid to the Ukrainian government.
Italy is also committed to encouraging Ukraine's entry into the European Union and NATO.
It then proposes the possibility of training the Ukrainian army and conducting exercises by the Italian army also on Ukrainian territory. In essence, it does not pay for having burned excellent decades-old relations with Russia, of having thrown an indefinite number of billions (the numbers are secreted) in the war support for Ukraine, for having contributed to an explosion in energy prices that has impoverished the country and continued in the activity of deindustrialization, now Giorgia Meloni wants to leave her mark in history by linking Italy more and more closely to a country that is plummeting, militarily and economically, exposing it in a growing way on the war. In fact, an article like the one that commits Italy to an immediate response of support in the event of a "new Russian attack" is either a joke pour parler (and then it is a shame) or it is a commitment that simulates Article 5 of the Atlantic Alliance, and then it is a suicide.
In addition, the idea of sending "Italian instructors" to train the Ukrainian army would be ridiculous (the war experience of the Ukrainians is now eaten for breakfast the Italian one), if it were not an informal way of sending operational troops. We remember that in the Vietnam War for many years the USA officially had only "military instructors and advisors" (tens of thousands) on the territory, with the official task of training the local anti-communist forces. And it is technically impossible to distinguish between an ordinary military and a military with tasks of 'instructing and advising'.
The idea then of supporting Ukraine's entry into NATO, although not new, reopens the wound of what from the beginning was the casus belli with Russia, which after seeing a continuous expansion east of NATO had placed a repeated veto on the fatal step of the Ukrainian entry into a hostile military alliance (Ukraine has the widest and most permeable of the borders, the one from which in history came the greatest threats to the existence of the Russian state.) The whole operation is seasoned with the usual rain of public money to lose, which will only partly fall into orders for the national war industry. In essence, once again, Giorgia Meloni is contributing (like many before her, it must be said) to a direct transfer of public resources from Italian schools and hospitals, for which the blanket is always very short, to the usual gang of oligarchs friends of friends. And this involves all Italians in a role of enemies of a great nuclear power.

‼️Here, we write this while waiting to see if the "list of Putinians in Italy" that Zelensky promised to give to the Italian premier to sanction them has already arrived at its destination.
Already because, for those who have forgotten, all this national demeal is done in the name of freedom and democracy.
That we are taught by those who proudly claim the legacy of the SS Galizien. (Andrea Zhok)

Today, the #Kremlin uses Azerbaijan as an instrument of military pressure against Armenia to prevent #Yerevan from cooperating with the West. Azerbaijan deliberately obstructs the signing of the peace treaty to preserve Russia's high influence in the region. If the conflict is resolved, Russia will not be able to maintain its influence in the #Caucasus at the expense of the blood of Armenians and Azerbaijanis.

A real hybrid war is being waged against Armenia to prevent actions to the West. While Azerbaijan and Russia are close allies. In other words, Russia allied with its "ally" Armenia's enemy, Azerbaijan. From my point of view, Moscow and Baku have no right to hinder the deepening of cooperation between Armenia and the West. 

A few days ago, on February 22, the 2nd year of the signing of the declaration confirming the military-political alliance signed by the presidents of Russia and Azerbaijan was completed. Two days before attacking Ukraine, Putin signed a military-political alliance with Azerbaijan, with whom he planned and implemented the war against #NagornoKarabakh in 2020. 

Russian peacekeepers have been deployed in Karabakh. With the February 22 alliance, Aliyev and Putin raised their countries' relations to the level of an alliance.

The document of friendship and cooperation signed on July 3, 1997, and the declaration of friendship and strategic partnership signed in 2008 were replaced by the military-political alliance. In other words, Russian-Azerbaijani relations are currently at the highest level.

With the declaration of February 22, "Russia and Azerbaijan undertook not to interfere in each other's affairs, to refrain from any action that one of the parties believes harms the strategic partnership and alliance relations of the two states. Russia fulfilled this obligation in #Karabakh by allowing the forced deportation of 120,000 Armenians of Karabakh."

According to Article 11, "Russia and Azerbaijan resolutely restrain the activities of organizations and individuals in their territories, directed against the state sovereignty, independence, and territorial integrity of the other party."

Russia remained faithful to this article, handing over to Azerbaijan in September 2023 the military and political leaders of Karabakh, who are currently in prison in #Baku. 

In other words, Moscow helped Baku in the fight against separatism. Karabakh doesn't exist today because of Russia.

Russia and Azerbaijan also agreed to hold operational and combat training events between the armed forces of the two countries, to cooperate in the military spheres, to cooperate in equipping with modern weapons and military equipment, and in other spheres.

In 2022, Aliyev and Putin agreed to intensify efforts to create maintenance, repair, and modernization service centers for weapons and military equipment, as well as to organize the joint production of various types of military products. 

According to Article 16 of the Alliance Declaration, Azerbaijan and Russia may consider the possibility of providing military assistance to each other.

In this sense, Azerbaijan has recently provided great military support to Russia, which is at war against Ukraine. In September 2023, after disarming the Nagorno-Karabakh Defense Army, and seizing weapons worth about 1 billion dollars from the Karabakh authorities, Azerbaijan handed them over to Russia. Today, Russia uses that weapon against #Ukraine.

In other words, although 67% of Azerbaijan's military arsenal consisted of lethal weapons sold by Russia and #Belarus in 2011-2020, now it is Azerbaijan that has provided military support to its military-political ally Russia. 

It is noteworthy that although there are almost no Armenians left in Karabakh today, Aliyev does not demand the removal of the Russians. This is done to prevent the possible entry of Western peacekeepers. Russia has provided great support to Baku, ensuring that Karabakh becomes Azerbaijani.

Russia and Azerbaijan signed an agreement on the exchange of intelligence data, aimed at protecting the territorial integrity of both states. This has already had extremely negative consequences for Armenia. The head of the Russian Foreign Intelligence Service, Sergey Naryshkin, said in Baku in July 2022:

"Our programs include joint actions aimed at supporting the protection of the national sovereignty and territorial integrity of our states, the joint fight against international terrorism and extremism, and the protection of the interests of our institutions abroad, citizens of Russia and Azerbaijan in third countries."

On September 13, 2022, Azerbaijan launched a military attack against the sovereign territories of Armenia, occupying an area of 200 square kilometers. Azerbaijan is an occupying country and continues to occupy territories of Armenia, including the lands of the four villages of Tavush and the Artsvashen enclave, totaling 200 square kilometers. 

Azerbaijan's attack was well planned, and on September 13, the Russian servicemen stationed on the border of Armenia left the Armenian #military bases entrusted to them and handed them over to Azerbaijan. Undoubtedly, the Russians knew in advance about Azerbaijan's attack and deliberately handed over the bases of Armenia to Azerbaijan. 

There is data that the Russians also have recruited intelligence information for Azerbaijan from the front line of Armenia. Basically, Russian servicemen are busy spying for Azerbaijan in Armenia.

In April 2023, Azerbaijan attacked the village of Tegh in Armenia and occupied territories. During this incident, Russian servicemen again left the Armenian positions entrusted to them, handing them over to the Azerbaijani army. Azerbaijan occupied the territory of Armenia with a length of about 5 kilometers. 

On February 13, 2024, Russian servicemen did not prevent the attack of Azerbaijan in Inner Hand. On September 19, 2023, the Russians handed over Karabakh to Azerbaijan. In other words, in all cases, Russia handed over intelligence data on Armenia to Azerbaijan and contributed to the occupation of Armenian territories.

With the Alliance Declaration signed in February 2022, Russia and Azerbaijan agreed to jointly fight against extremism and separatism. Recently, the Russian ambassador to Azerbaijan announced that Azerbaijan has carried out an anti-terrorist operation in Nagorno-Karabakh. In other words, on November 9, 2020, Putin decided to send troops to Karabakh to "protect the terrorist Armenians?

This is the logic of short-sighted Russia. And recently, the Russian Foreign Ministry announced that there is no international document confirming the forced deportation of Karabakh. Russia is trying to avoid its responsibility, to put the blame on Armenia, which did not work.

In other words, by handing over Karabakh to Azerbaijan, leaving 120,000 Armenians defenseless against the Azerbaijani troops, Russia fulfilled the above-mentioned points of the #Aliyev-#Putin alliance declaration. 

Russia and Azerbaijan fought against separatism in the face of Karabakh. I'm just sorry that Karabakh Armenians realized this late. Russia sold them without thinking for a second. They and the Armenian authorities did not want to notice how Russia and Azerbaijan became allies.

How Russia sold billions of dollars worth of weapons to Azerbaijan remained unnoticed. As I mentioned at the beginning, Russia has chosen Azerbaijan as an ally in the South Caucasus. This has completely freed the hands of Armenia, which has to draw up plans and sign documents on serious military-political cooperation with the #USA, #France, and the #EU. 

Hopefully, we will witness such a process. Deep cooperation with the West and establishment of alliance relations are the main factors that will strengthen Armenia's statehood and sovereignty. Russia has changed its foreign policy vector in the #SouthCaucasus, and Armenia is obliged to record the fact of Russia's disloyalty and betrayal. 

The priority is serving the state interests of Armenia, and Russia is strictly absent from that plan. Moreover, Russia has ceded the role of Armenia's ally to the West. We will see how the processes will go.

1 Medicaid expansion will cost state nothing: analysis

The Kansas Health Institute on Thursday unveiled its analysis of Gov. Laura Kelly’s proposal to expand Medicaid, predicting 152,000 Kansans would enroll in the first year with no additional cost to the state government. The Democratic governor has made passage of Medicaid expansion a top legislative priority this year, following her statewide campaign to promote the policy last fall. But Republican leadership in the Legislature opposes the policy and has blocked hearings on Medicaid expansion for four years.
KS Reflector
https://t.co/nv4QQ3bPEY

2 KS House passes unemployment reforms

Kansas House of Representatives introduced House Bill 2570 on January 23, 2024, which proposed pausing state unemployment insurance benefits when the federal government creates temporary benefit supplements—such as the $600 weekly stipends issued by the federal government during the COVID-19 pandemic.

The bill also proposed the following:

Requiring prospective employers to disclose the identities of applicants who miss job interviews without notification.
Defining temporary unemployment insurance benefits as benefits claimed by employees temporarily laid off from a job they expect to resume in under four weeks.
Authorizing the secretary of labor to extend temporary unemployment insurance benefits upon request by employers who expect a layoff to last longer than four weeks.
Creating an audit process for work search activities in the state’s unemployment insurance information technology system.

Unemployment insurance is a joint federal and state program that provides temporary monetary benefits to eligible laid-off workers who are actively seeking new employment. Qualifying individuals receive unemployment compensation as a percentage of their lost wages in the form of weekly cash benefits while they search for new employment.

The bill passed the House 120-0 Feb 22.

Center Square #ksleg
https://t.co/ii7FKNl1ve

3 Police drone aids in fugitive pursuit

Just after 9p.m. Tuesday, police were dispatched to a report of gunshots in the 700 block of Neosho Street in Emporia, according to Police Captain Ray Mattas. Several officers went to locate the source of the gunfire and located a man later identified as 33-year-old Juan Cervantes Jr. in the area of the gunfire. Cervantes ran from the officers and was found again and arrested. In the 700 block of Rural, according to Mattas. Officers also located several shell casings. A Lyon County Sheriff’s Office deputy, using a drone, located a firearm that had been discarded. Cervantes is being held in the Lyon County Jail on requested charges of Felon in Possession of a Firearm, Unlawful Discharge of a Firearm, Interference with a Law Enforcement Officer, Possession of Marijuana, and Possession of Drug Paraphernalia.
Salina Post
https://t.co/U91O3oH0or

4 KS refugee story: Africa to Wichita

I was born in a refugee camp in Lusaka City, Zambia, on Jan. 1, 2000. My mum and my brothers had run from conflicts in the the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and went to Zambia to seek refuge.

While in Zambia in 2017, my mother got killed by thugs in Lusaka City.

My mother applied for us to come to America before she passed away. After this, we waited close to six years before being resettled in the USA.

They have called us, the immigration, America called us, we are going there. They said, we can take your whole family at one time, but it will take you longer. After that, they started taking us one by one. That’s how I find myself alone here. When I reached Texas, they said you are going to Wichita, Kansas.

My brothers and my sister and my uncle, they are in one place. They are all in Philadelphia, but in different houses. Since last year, I have not seen them. Only phone calls and video calls.

I came here on June 2, 2023. I found myself going to Creekstone Farms to work, cutting meat. But Creekstone is very far. We’d take like one hour on the road to reach there. There was no way I could go to school, because we had to wake up around 03 (3 a.m.), the buses start going around 04 (4 a.m.) and then you come back around 18 (6 p.m.). That was my first job in America. My dream is to be a nurse, I would love to do like a midwife, that is what I would love. If I didn’t do that, I would like to do nursing assistant. Some of my church members helped me to find another job, because I told them I need to go back to school; if I could find any job that can suit me going to my job and then going to school, it would be so good for me. So they told me, OK, when Dold Foods (in Wichita) opens they would try to do so. And then I was lucky, they did so and then they called me there and interviewed me, and then they hired me. I chose the night shift so I could go to school in the evening and in the afternoon. I work 22 (10 p.m.) to 06 (6 a.m.). I have somebody who picks me up. We work together and she lives nearby. I’m trying to find a school now. I need to get a GED because I didn’t finish (high school), so I need to get a diploma, before I can go to nursing school.
Wichita Eagle
https://t.co/EeXwGKxB5F

text:
🇵🇼 🇵🇹 Palau, Portugal find common ground 
(our ocean) for strengthened cooperation

New Portugal Ambassador to Palau H.E. Antonio Manuel Albuquerque presented his credentials to President Surangel S. Whipps, Jr. on Tuesday, Feb. 26.

The ambassador, who is based in Canberra, Australia, noted Palau’s leadership in environmental stewardship, which he described as similar to his country’s strong record on the environment.

“Like Palau, Portugal is very oriented to the sea,” the ambassador said, adding that while his country is one of the smallest in Europe in terms of land area, it has the largest marine territory of the continent. “Palau has a long tradition of investing in the ocean, the environment.”  

A shared concern discussed was mining the seabed, an issue coming to the fore in the international community as plans are being finalized to commence mining activities by certain countries. The Ambassador said there are many universities and ocean engineers in Portugal studying seabed mining and working on ocean protection.

Palau introduced the Alliance of Countries Calling for a Deep-Sea Mining Moratorium at the 2022 UN Ocean Conference in Lisbon. Fiji and Samoa were the first countries to join the Alliance, followed by the Federated States of Micronesia.

text:
#Ethiopia and #Somaliland are joining forces to enhance their cooperation in the health sector: 

Dr. Ayele Teshome, (@ayumesif ) Ethiopia's state minister of health, and Liban Yousuf Osman, ( @DeputyLiban ) Somaliland's deputy minister of health, held a fruitful meeting in #Addis_Abeba today. 
They discussed ways to strengthen their existing health collaboration and explored new areas of cooperation. 
The two countries are eager to expand their partnership in various fields, including nutrition, emergency preparedness, cross-border health care coordination, and experience sharing.

Acting Minister of Foreign Affairs Mawlawi Amir Khan Muttaqi and his delegation met with the Deputy Minister of the Council of Ministers and Minister of Foreign Affairs, Mr. Rashid Muradov.
During this meeting, the two sides discussed bilateral cooperation between #Afghanistan and #Turkmenistan, increasing Ashgabat's diplomatic presence in Afghanistan, the practical initiation of the #TAPI project in the country, enhancing the electricity import from Turkmenistan to #Herat province, the establishment of the Lajward Route, the development of the Afghan railway, and Turkmenistan's investment in Afghanistan. Mawlawi Amir Khan Muttaqi announced the Islamic Emirate's readiness to commence practical work on the TAPI project in Afghanistan, stating that this project will soon begin with the collaborative efforts of both countries. He emphasized the Islamic Emirate's focus on regional economic connectivity and highlighted the construction of railways and highways as key priorities. He urged the Turkmen side to invest in the development of the Turghandi-Herat and Andkhoi-Shabarghan railways to Mazar-e-Sharif. Additionally, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Turkmenistan expressed that expanding trade with Afghanistan is a top priority for his country, and the practical work on the TAPI project will soon commence through joint efforts in Afghanistan.

text:
#Philippines fortifying a #Taiwan front line against #China

Northernmost Batanes facilities and ports set for significant upgrades as US-Philippine cooperation builds for a potential Taiwan war.

“Our relationship with [China] is one of intense strategic competition. At the same time, the United States is committed to managing this competition responsibly so that it does not veer into miscalculation or conflict,” said US Assistant Secretary of State for East Asian and Pacific Affairs Daniel Kritenbrink at a recent Atlantic Council think tank event.

“We don’t want [developing] countries to have to choose between us and [China]. But we want to help ensure that they have a choice and that they can make their decisions free from coercion,” he added, referring to Global South nations navigating increasingly contentious relations between the superpowers.

Speaking at the same event, veteran Singaporean diplomat Bilahari Kausikan arguedthat Southeast Asian nations, in particular, will refuse to side with any superpower against the other since they “have concerns about both certain aspects of Chinese behavior and certain aspects of American behavior.”

The situation in the Philippines, however, is unique. The Southeast Asian nation is not only a full-fledged US defense treaty ally but it is also increasingly contributing to an emerging “integrated deterrence” strategy against China.

In particular, the Philippines is expanding its military presence in its northernmost islands, which are situated close to Taiwan’s southern shores.

Earlier this month, Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro visited a naval detachment in Mavulis, situated only 88 miles from the southern tip of Taiwan and even closer to many nearby Taiwanese islands.

The outspoken Philippine defense chief, who has openly questioned the value of diplomatic engagement with Beijing, has provocatively described the nation’s northernmost military facility as the “spearhead of the Philippines.”

Crucially, Manila and Washington are also exploring a joint presence in the area under the expanded Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement (EDCA) – a development that would constitute a potential “redline’’ for China.

Although still hedging his great power bets, Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr has admitted that it’s “very hard to imagine” for his country to remain totally “neutral” amid ongoing fears of a Chinese invasion of Taiwan.

Reverse island chain

Marcos Jr’s decision to fortify bilateral defense cooperation with the US, barely a month after a state visit to Beijing, was doubly surprising.

For starters, it ran counter to his initial push for a “new golden era” of bilateral relations with China and his longtime emphasis on diplomacy and balanced relations with superpowers.

But even more surprising has been his decision to expand the parameters of the EDCA with a northern orientation toward Taiwan rather than a western one towards the South China Sea, where Manila and Beijing are at heated loggerheads over contested territories and features.

The bulk of the new bases under the expanded EDCA are located in northernmost Philippine provinces, namely Cagayan and Isabela, which are notably distant from the South China Sea theater.

Crucially, the two allies have also expanded joint exercises with a growing focus on the Philippines’ northern shores.

The US is also looking at developing a port facility in the Philippines’ northernmost province of Batanes, which includes Mavulis. Last year, officials from the US Pentagon were reportedly in Batanes, where they discussed funding for the project with Governor Marilou Cayco.

Philippine authorities maintain that any cooperation with the Pentagon is primarily geared toward economic and civilian purposes. 

The port in Batanes, for instance, is supposed to enhance connectivity between the relatively isolated province – surrounded by rough seas and regularly battered by monsoons – and the rest of the Philippines.

As for facilities in other northern Philippine provinces, authorities insist that any US support or rotational presence will be primarily for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) operations.

But as one Washington-based observer told this author, those facilities could also be “dual-use.” For instance, EDCA facilities built for potentially housing HADR-related hardware such as cargo planes could also, in theory, host fighter jets and more offensive-oriented weapons systems.

But as Steve Tsang, director of the China Institute at London’s School of Oriental and Africa Studies, recently told the media, “Beijing will see any US move to build up ports and facilities…[both] overtly military ones or ostensibly civilian ones” in the Indo-Pacific as a hostile act as they can also “be used to support any potential US intervention over a Chinese use of force against Taiwan as hostile.”

In particular, China fears that the US is trying to build an “island chain” of dual-purpose facilities around Taiwan to more effectively respond to any potential continency over the self-ruling island that China views as a renegade province that must eventually be “reunified” with the mainland.

Mixed strategic signals

For now, the Marcos Jr administration has been sending mixed signals on its stance on the Taiwan question. During his trip to Washington last year, the Filipino president equivocated on his country’s potential contribution to an “integrated deterrence” strategy on Taiwan.

In contrast to top Philippine military officials, who publicly admitted the possibility of joint operations during a contingency, Marcos Jr has been adamant that the country’s defense posture is primarily “defensive” and not pointed at any particular country, namely China.

Nevertheless, the trajectory of the Philippine-US defense cooperation in recent months suggests that the Southeast Asian nation is gradually preparing for a D-Day, both in its western and northern waters.

Filipino strategists believe China is increasingly treating the Bashi Channel, the South China Sea and Taiwan Strait as part of an integrated theater. That, they say, is driving the need for correspondingly comprehensive countermeasures as the nation shifts its strategic posture and overall defense calculus vis-à-vis China.

“Starting 2024, the operational tempo for the AFP (Armed Forces of the Philippines) will be higher,” the Philippine defense chief said during his recent visit to the Philippines’ northernmost military facility in Batanes. “[Batanes is the] spearhead of the Philippines as far as the northern baseline is concerned,” he added.

The Philippine Navy described the unprecedented visit by the Philippines’ defense chief, who was accompanied by the AFP chief and top admiral, as signifying “a pivotal moment in our nation’s commitment to territorial defense and national security.”

Last November, the US and Philippines conducted joint patrols in the area and this year are set to stage even larger joint drills near Batanes. China has warned the Philippines against “stoking the fire” by expanding its military presence and joint activities with the US in the area.

But top Filipino strategists believe that the country has no choice but to press on, given the extremely high stakes.

“My worst case scenario is China’s [occupation of] Taiwan since the [self-ruling island] is essentially our buffer state,” Rommel Jude Ong, a retired rear admiral, told the author in a recent interview.

text:
In 2024, russia plans to produce 2.7 million shells — Vadym Skibitskyi, Deputy Chief of the Defence Intelligence of Ukraine.

In an interview with Interfax-Ukraine, Vadym Skibitskyi spoke about the state of the russian military industry, cooperation between Moscow and Pyongyang in the field of armaments, and the role of F-16 aircraft, which are very much feared in russia.
 
Highlights from the conversation:  
 
In February 2024, russians planned to launch 130 missiles of the Iskander, Kalibr, X-101, X-32, and Kinzhal types.
 
A specialized agency has been set up in tussia to replace foreign components, especially electronic components, with russian-made ones. They are of poorer quality, not as perfect, but they allow for the production of weapons.
 
Currently, russians are using missiles manufactured in the fourth quarter of 2023, which means that they are just off the assembly line. We see that the missiles no longer meet the announced characteristics, their quality is deteriorating.
 
Last year, russia produced about 2 million 122 mm and 152 mm shells.
 
In 2024, russians plan to produce 2.7 million shells. We'll see whether these plans will be realized, as they require modernization of production, decommissioning, or the creation of new lines.
 
russians have already taken out all the ammunition from Belarus, so there is nothing left to take from there.
 
russians have already received 1.5 million 122 mm and 152 mm shells from North Korea. These are 70-80-year-old ammunition, and half of them fail to work.
 
Pyongyang seeks to receive military technologies from Moscow in exchange for the supply of shells, missiles and other weapons to develop its military-industrial complex.
 
In particular, North Korea asks the Kremlin for technologies related to nuclear weapons. russia must be held accountable for this.
 
Nuclear blackmail is a very powerful tool used by russia to influence political processes, particularly in Europe.
 
russia, the aggressor state, has about 300 combat aircraft against us.
 
The F-16 is one of the tools that will allow, among other things, to destroy tussian aircraft used for bombing. This means that there will no longer be 75 enemy bombs falling on Avdiivka in a single day, or on Krynyky, where the russians dropped, for instance, 35-45 bombs on one of our small foothold.
 
The Kremlin is very afraid that powerful Western equipment will come to Ukraine, so the aggressor has set itself the task of destroying both our aviation and infrastructure.
 
russia is forming a so-called "volunteer corps" of 18,000 people, including former Wagnerites. This process is managed by the Ministry of Defense of tussia. These "veterans' brigades" operate according to the Wagner principle — constant assaults and achieving results at any cost.
 
Foreigners recruited by russia, in particular Africans and Syrians, go to war only to desert at the first opportunity and then get to Europe.

Source of highlights: Ukrainian Defense Intelligence

text:
In 2024, Russia plans to produce 2.7 million shells — Vadym Skibitskyi, Deputy Chief of the Defence Intelligence of Ukraine.

In an interview with Interfax-Ukraine, Vadym Skibitskyi spoke about the state of the Russian military industry, cooperation between Moscow and Pyongyang in the field of armaments, and the role of F-16 aircraft, which are very much feared in Russia.
 
 Highlights from the conversation:  
 
▪️ In February 2024, Russians planned to launch 130 missiles of the Iskander, Kalibr, X-101, X-32, and Kinzhal types.
 
▪️ A specialized agency has been set up in Russia to replace foreign components, especially electronic components, with Russian-made ones. They are of poorer quality, not as perfect, but they allow for the production of weapons.
 
▪️ Currently, Russians are using missiles manufactured in the fourth quarter of 2023, which means that they are just off the assembly line. We see that the missiles no longer meet the announced characteristics, their quality is deteriorating.
 
▪️ Last year, Russia produced about 2 million 122 mm and 152 mm shells.
 
▪️ In 2024, Russians plan to produce 2.7 million shells. We'll see whether these plans will be realized, as they require modernization of production, decommissioning, or the creation of new lines.
 
▪️ Russians have already taken out all the ammunition from Belarus, so there is nothing left to take from there.
 
▪️ Russians have already received 1.5 million 122 mm and 152 mm shells from North Korea. These are 70-80-year-old ammunition, and half of them fail to work.
 
▪️ Pyongyang seeks to receive military technologies from Moscow in exchange for the supply of shells, missiles and other weapons to develop its military-industrial complex.
 
▪️ In particular, North Korea asks the Kremlin for technologies related to nuclear weapons. Russia must be held accountable for this.
 
▪️ Nuclear blackmail is a very powerful tool used by Russia to influence political processes, particularly in Europe.
 
▪️ Russia, the aggressor state, has about 300 combat aircraft against us.
 
▪️ The F-16 is one of the tools that will allow, among other things, to destroy Russian aircraft used for bombing. This means that there will no longer be 75 enemy bombs falling on Avdiivka in a single day, or on Krynyky, where the Russians dropped, for instance, 35-45 bombs on one of our small foothold.
 
▪️ The Kremlin is very afraid that powerful Western equipment will come to Ukraine, so the aggressor has set itself the task of destroying both our aviation and infrastructure.
 
▪️ Russia is forming a so-called "volunteer corps" of 18,000 people, including former Wagnerites. This process is managed by the Ministry of Defense of Russia. These "veterans' brigades" operate according to the Wagner principle — constant assaults and achieving results at any cost.
 
▪️ Foreigners recruited by Russia, in particular Africans and Syrians, go to war only to desert at the first opportunity and then get to Europe.

Source of highlights: Ukrainian Defense Intelligence Telegram channel
